# host_file_block (Resource)

Manages one Terraform-owned content block inside a named `host_file` section.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "host_file" "zshrc" {
  path   = "~/.zshrc"
  render = "clean"

  block = {
    alias = {}
  }
}

resource "host_file_block" "foo_alias" {
  file_block = host_file.zshrc.block["alias"]
  content    = "alias foo=foobar"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `content` (String) Content to place inside the target file section.
- `file_block` (Attributes) Target file section, usually `host_file.<name>.block["section"]`. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--file_block))

### Optional

- `priority` (Number) Sort priority within the target file section. Blocks are ordered by `priority`, then `content`.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) Stable identifier for this content block.

<a id="nestedatt--file_block"></a>
### Nested Schema for `file_block`

Required:

- `name` (String) Target file section name.
- `path` (String) Path to the host file that contains the target section.

Optional:

- `render` (String) Render mode inherited from the target host file section.
